chapter_title.ar: باب بَيْعِ الزَّرْعِ الْأَخْضَرِ وَ الْقَصِيلِ وَ أَشْبَاهِهِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 133 – Selling the green plants, and the straw, and such like

text: عُثْمَانُ بْنُ عِيسَى عَنْ سَمَاعَةَ قَالَ سَأَلْتُهُ عَنْ رَجُلٍ زَرَعَ زَرْعاً مُسْلِماً كَانَ أَوْ مُعَاهَداً فَأَنْفَقَ فِيهِ نَفَقَةً ثُمَّ بَدَا لَهُ فِي بَيْعِهِ لِنَقْلِهِ يَنْتَقِلُ مِنْ مَكَانِهِ أَوْ لِحَاجَةٍ قَالَ يَشْتَرِيهِ بِالْوَرِقِ فَإِنَّ أَصْلَهُ طَعَامٌ .

en.hubeali: Usman Bin Isa, from Sama’at who said,

‘I asked him<sup>asws</sup> about a man who cultivated a farm of one who was a Muslim or a confederate. So he spent therein some expenditure, then it became inevitable for him to sell it due to his relocation from his place, or a need of his. He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘He should buy it with the silver (Dirhams), for its origin is foodstuff’.
